edge_hypothesis: Vortex transition is the champion family (PF=2.034) because it detects regime transitions via directional-movement crossover. But is transition detection a GENERAL alpha source, or a Vortex-specific artifact? Ichimoku Tenkan-Kijun (ITS_9/IKS_26) is a DIFFERENT transition signal — median-price lookbacks over 9/26 bars vs Vortex's directional-movement ratio. If Ichimoku TK transition also works, it CONFIRMS that 'regime transition detection' is a universal edge mechanism, not indicator-specific. This would be the most important finding since discovering Vortex — it would mean we can mine MORE transition signals from other indicator families. Supertrend direction confirms the transition is aligned with the broader trend structure, filtering counter-trend whipsaws.
target_regime: transitional (TK cross fires at regime changes) + trending (Supertrend confirmation filters for trend alignment)

variants.name: ichimoku_tk_supertrend_10to1
variants.description: Tenkan-Kijun cross confirmed by Supertrend direction at 10:1 R:R. Direct test of whether transition detection generalizes beyond Vortex. Uses Vortex v3a stop/TP geometry (1.0/10) since we're testing the same regime-transition hypothesis.

components.notes: ITS_9 (Tenkan) crosses IKS_26 (Kijun) — median-price transition signal. NEVER TESTED in any strategy.
SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction confirms trend alignment, filters counter-trend whipsaws

variants.name: ichimoku_tk_cloud_8to1
variants.description: Tenkan-Kijun cross confirmed by cloud position (close above Senkou A for long, below Senkou B for short) at 8:1 R:R. Pure Ichimoku strategy — no Supertrend dependency. Tests whether Ichimoku's internal cloud confirmation is sufficient without external indicator. Wider stop (1.5 ATR) because cloud entries are slightly later than raw TK cross.

components.notes: Close vs Senkou Span A/B (ISA_9/ISB_26) — Ichimoku cloud as trend direction filter

variants.name: ichimoku_tk_chop_transition_10to1
variants.description: Tenkan-Kijun cross with CHOP < 50 gate at 10:1 R:R. CHOP < 50 selects bars where a trend is actively forming. Combined with TK cross, this should capture the exact moment a ranging market breaks into a trend — the transition sweet spot. Control variant against Supertrend-confirmed version.
